Container.Name: freetube-web
Container.Overview:
FreeTube Web — a self-hosted, privacy-focused YouTube frontend.
Watch YouTube without ads or tracking, from any browser on your network. All data (subscriptions, watch history, playlists, settings) is stored in the browser's IndexedDB — there is no server-side database.
Multi-user: set FREETUBE_USERS to a comma-separated list of names (e.g. alice,bob) to show a profile picker. Each user gets their own isolated data in the browser.
Based on FreeTube (https://github.com/FreeTubeApp/FreeTube). Licensed under AGPL-3.0-or-later.
Container.Requires: No additional containers required. No volumes or path mappings needed — all user data lives in the browser.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: Port to access FreeTube Web in your browser. Map your preferred host port to the container's internal port.

Config.Name: User Profiles
Config.Description: Comma-separated list of profile names shown on the 'Who's watching?' picker screen. Use a single name (e.g. 'default') to skip the picker and go straight to the app. Example: alice,bob,carol. Names are lowercased and restricted to letters, digits, hyphens, and underscores.

Config.Name: Internal Port
Config.Description: The port the Node.js server listens on inside the container. Change only if you need a non-standard internal port; must match the container side of the port mapping above.

Config.Name: Node Environment
Config.Description: Set to 'production' for normal use. Set to 'development' to enable detailed error messages in the server logs (not recommended for long-term use).
